east-village.note: N/R/W from Union Sq or 8 St run direct to Herald Sq; the 6 now leaves a crosstown walk from Park Ave, so the L + 14 St transfer covers the Ave A end.

lower-east-side.note: F from 2 Av and M from Essex run direct to Herald Sq; J/Z need a transfer — and the stations still sit at the neighborhood's edges, so count the walk.

williamsburg.note: M direct to Herald Sq from the Marcy/Hewes side; L + 14 St transfer from Bedford. Ferry is still the scenic backup, not the fast one.

east-williamsburg.note: L + 14 St transfer is the default on a train already full when it reaches you; near the Flushing Av edge the M runs direct to Herald Sq and skips the transfer.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq, then E to Penn or 7 to Times Sq — the transfer is unavoidable, plan +10 min.

bushwick.note: M now runs direct to Herald Sq — the headline win; L or J/Z still mean a transfer, and the deeper into Bushwick you live, the longer the ride.

astoria.note: N/W direct to Herald Sq or Times Sq — same no-transfer elevated ride, then a short walk to 37th.

long-island-city.note: E drops you at Penn two blocks from the door; 7 to Times Sq and M to Herald Sq also run direct. The G covers your Brooklyn trips.

hells-kitchen.note: Now a 15-20 min walk from the upper 40s and 50s, or a one-stop A/C/E or 1 hop to Penn on rainy days.

yorkville.note: Q direct to Times Sq and Herald Sq is the play; the Lex 4/5/6 now mean the 42 St shuttle or a long crosstown walk.

harlem.note: 2/3 from the Lenox Ave stops run express below 96 St straight to Times Sq and Penn; A/D from 125 St on the St. Nicholas side are even faster. The ride is short — it is the walk to your nearest spine that decides the door-to-door.

ridgewood.note: M from Forest Av or Fresh Pond Rd runs direct to Herald Sq — no transfer, just a long ride; from the Halsey St end the L means switching to the M at Myrtle-Wyckoff or transferring at 14 St.

crown-heights.note: 2/3 from the Eastern Parkway stops run direct to Penn, a block from the door — roughly 33 min from Kingston Av; the 4/5 are faster trains to the wrong avenue, and blocks east of Kingston add real minutes.

prospect-heights.note: Two genuine no-transfer routes — 2/3 from Grand Army Plaza or Bergen St direct to Penn, B/Q from Seven Av to Herald Sq; rare luck for brownstone Brooklyn.

fort-greene.note: C from Lafayette Av runs local but direct to Penn; the Atlantic Av-Barclays hub on the south edge adds N/Q/R to Herald Sq and Times Sq. The G is for Brooklyn crosstown errands, not this commute.

bed-stuy.note: A express from Nostrand or Utica Av reaches Penn in under 30 — quick for this far out; the C crawls the same track, and the G and J/Z edges of the neighborhood mean a transfer. Pick your block by the A.

chelsea.note: From the 20s it is a 10-20 min walk up 7th or 8th Ave; the 1 from 18/23/28 St or the A/C/E to Penn covers weather days — the office is barely outside the neighborhood.

west-village.note: 1 from Christopher St runs straight up 7th Ave to Penn; 2/3 express or A/C/E from 14 St shave it further. Sub-15 most days — the catch in the Village is the rent, not the ride.
